headline: Michigan seeks reset against ranked Oregon in must-win road test
subhead: The Wolverines arrive in Eugene desperate to halt a four-game skid against a Ducks team riding momentum after a statement win over Clemson.

xFactor.why: Michigan's defense must establish early pressure and force Oregon into three-and-outs to keep pace with the Ducks' improved offensive rhythm.

keyMatchup: Oregon's pass rush versus Michigan's offensive line will determine whether the Wolverines can sustain drives and avoid falling into a scoring deficit they cannot erase.
body: Michigan arrives at Autzen Stadium with its season hanging by a thread, having surrendered 36 points to Boston College just days ago. The Wolverines rank 15th nationally but play like a team searching for answers on both sides of the ball, while Oregon enters this matchup with legitimate confidence after outpacing Clemson in a primetime showcase. The talent disparity is narrower than the records suggest, but Oregon's recent offensive execution and home-field advantage create a tilted playing field.

The coaching room in Ann Arbor must find a way to limit explosive plays and control tempo, forcing Oregon into long drives that eat clock and increase the margin for error. Oregon's defense has shown vulnerability in stretches, and Michigan's skill position players possess the talent to exploit it, but they will need to avoid the stalled drives and red-zone mistakes that have plagued them this season.
